channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Arizona's Democratic governor faces uphill battle as Republicans keep tight grip on legislature
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Arizona Democratic Gov. Katie Hobbs knows she is outnumbered this legislative session, with Republicans having expanded their majorities in the statehouse after the last election.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CA firefighters face impersonators, staffing shortages, low pay
item.description: LA County Sheriff Robert Luna said he encountered a man dressed as a firefighter burglarizing a home.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Vance says Jan. 6 participants who committed violence 'obviously' shouldn't be pardoned
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance says people responsible for the violence during the Capitol riot “obviously” should not be pardoned, as President-elect Donald Trump is promising to use his clemency power on behalf of many of those who tried on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom defends fire response with fact-check site linking to Dem Party fundraising platform
item.description: Democratic California Gov. Gavin Newsom is combating "misinformation" surrounding his handling of the fires with a website that asks for donations through ActBlue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans urge Trump nominees: Don't take the bait from Democrats
item.description: Senate Republicans are urging President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees to resist taking the bait from Democrats when their high-stakes confirmation hearings kick off next week, marking a make-or-break moment for some of his choices.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: After the initial crush of personnel announcements for President-elect Donald Trump's incoming administration, now the nominations process officially begins. Senate hearings are scheduled this coming week for several of Trump's picks for the Cabinet. Many have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Biden joins the former presidents club, here are some ideas for his retirement to-do list
item.description: If President Joe Biden is looking for ideas for retirement, he need look no further than past presidents.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: DNC chair candidates attempt to stand out at first forum
item.description: The eight people vying for the position of chair were featured last and while there was no clear winner, there was definitely a loser.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans face massive debt problem
item.description: House Republicans have a problem. They want to pass a massive agenda for President-elect Trump, preferably in his first 100 days of office. And they don’t want to add to the federal deficit. That looks impossible.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Special counsel Jack Smith has resigned after submitting his Trump report, Justice Department says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Special counsel Jack Smith has resigned from the Justice Department after submitting his investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump, an expected move that comes amid legal wrangling over how much of that document can be made public in the days ahead.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Special counsel Jack Smith resigns after 2-year stint at Department of Justice
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith left his position at the Department of Justice Friday, according to a court document filed Saturday. He was hired by Merrick Garland in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Political experts discuss Trump-Newsom relationship, political fallout for LA Mayor Bass amid wildfires
item.description: (INSIDE CALIFORNIA POLITICS) — The Los Angeles County wildfires have claimed several lives and burned thousands of structures have also caused a stir in the political sphere. San Francisco Chronicle columnist Emily Hoeven spoke with Inside California Politics about how L.A.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina's new Democratic governor pledges to rebuild after Helene, work with GOP
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina Gov. Josh Stein kicked off his term in office Saturday by praising the resilience of the state's residents following Hurricane Helene and pledging to “cut through red tape to help people rebuild quicker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden honors Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Saturday honored Pope Francis with the Presidential Medal of Freedom with distinction, the highest civilian award given by the president, saying the pontiff was “a light of faith, hope, and love that shines brightly across the world.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors seek 15-year sentence for disgraced former NJ Senator Bob Menendez after bribery conviction
item.description: Prosecutors are seeking at 15-year sentence for disgraced former New Jersey Senator Bob Menendez, who was convicted in a bribery case after a nine-week-long trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Many Democrats don't think they'll see a woman become president, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Democrats are harboring strong feelings of stress and gloom as the new year begins. And many are questioning whether their party's commitment to diverse candidates — especially women — may lead to further political struggles in the Donald Trump era.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress braces for California wildfire fallout
item.description: House lawmakers are bracing for what could be a contentious battle over emergency spending in the wake of the wildfires that are tearing through Southern California — an unprecedented disaster that’s already estimated to have caused more than $50 billion in damage in and around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SALT-focused Republicans to meet with Trump as they threaten to hold up bill
item.description: Republicans who are pledging to withhold support for President-elect Trump’s ambitious agenda if the state and local tax (SALT) deduction cap is not raised are set to meet with the incoming president this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state by inserting Bible references into reading lessons and requiring teachers to post the Ten Commandments.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gov. Newsom cut fire budget by $100M months before lethal California fires: report
item.description: A review of California Gov. Newsom's budget cuts shows fire prevention funding was reduced substantially just months before the California fires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI must be independent and above the partisan fray, outgoing director says in farewell address
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The FBI must remain independent, above the partisan fray and “committed to upholding the rule of law,” outgoing Director Christopher Wray said in a farewell address just days before his expected retirement at the conclusion of the Biden administration following more than seven year
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Hochul defends congestion pricing, explains inflation rebate checks
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In a few days, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul will deliver her State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump fills latest Cabinet spots as Jan. 20 inauguration nears
item.description: President-elect Trump named Bill Briggs as Deputy Administrator of the U.S. Small Business Administration and Ed Russo as his pick for the Environmental Advisory Task Force.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court refuses Trump’s request to block Friday’s criminal sentencing
item.description: The Supreme Court in a 5-4 decision on Thursday refused President-elect Trump’s request to halt his criminal sentencing, now just hours away, closing off Trump’s last remaining pathway to avoid receiving his punishment for his 34-count felony conviction in New York.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams delivers 4th State of the City address
item.description: HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his fourth State of the City address at The Apollo in Harlem on Thursday. Adams discussed topics like public safety, the cost of living, and new initiatives to build more in the city.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: House Republican bill would pave way for Trump to acquire Panama Canal for US
item.description: Rep. Dusty Johnson (R-S.D.) will introduce a bill that would authorize the president to purchase the Panama Canal and put it under U.S. control, an acquisition that President-elect Trump has been pushing over the last several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: National Day of Mourning: What's closed as Jimmy Carter is laid to rest
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Some facilities and operations will be closed on Thursday in observance of the National Day of Mourning following former President Jimmy Carter's death. Here's what's closed: All flags will continue to be flown at half-staff for 30 days from Dec.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Board of Supervisors splits votes on new chair following Vargas' exit
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors punted a vote on who will succeed Nora Vargas as chair after competing motions to elevate either Terra Lawson-Remer or Jim Desmond to the role failed in votes down partisan lines.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: New California law aims to prevent book bans in libraries statewide
item.description: By 2026, libraries in San Diego must come up with a collection development policy that would outline how they choose what books they'll add to their shelves, as well as provide a way for readers to express their concerns about the library materials and giving them a way to request new ones.
item.dc:creator: Dominique LaVigne, Fox 5

item.title: Politicians reflect on January 6, shifting landscape of power transitions
item.description: The 2024 Presidential Election results have been officially certified by members of Congress. While the session typically happens without fanfare, there was extra security in place this time.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge holds Giuliani in contempt in Georgia election workers case
item.description: A federal judge held Rudy Giuliani in civil contempt on Monday for failing to comply with court orders in two former Georgia election workers’ efforts to collect their $146 million defamation judgment. At the end of a two-day hearing that saw Giuliani take the stand, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Pence: 'Admirable' that Harris certified Trump's win after losing election
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence, who had to be evacuated four years ago Monday as rioters stormed the Capitol, welcomed "the return of order and civility" as Congress moved to certify President-elect Donald Trump's 2024 victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
